highlights: Hub latest: c46a177 data: fleet usage update

cro.description: Growth strategy, monetization. Gazelle-intense focus on revenue.

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Evening, everyone. Friday night wrap — let's keep this tight. Here's where we stand. Build is still unhealthy. We've got 760 commits this week which is strong velocity, but that hub build failure is blocking us. Three critical ecosystem sync gaps remain. On the CEO side, Joshua confirmed tonight's session includes the Family tab spec, and he's standing by for the analytics merge once James validates. Jett's vaccine is done — that's closed. Let's go around. Elon, build status first. Elon (CTO): Build is broken and that's priority one. I've traced the issue — it's related to that inline shared package change in the monorepo, commit 465126b. I need to fix that tonight. We also have one uncommitted hub file sitting there and three critical sync gaps — podcast MDX mismatch, blog content gap, and the app still not on the App Store. I'll knock out the build fix and the uncommitted file, then address those sync gaps. The five attention items I can triage overnight as well. James (CTIO): Analytics Swift hooks PR is in final validation right now. I'm running the test suite clean — no flaky tests, no regressions. The moment it passes, I'll ping Joshua on Slack per his directive. If he merges tonight, the day-one patch ships with full analytics instrumentation baked in. That's the single most important thing we can do before users hit the app — we need data from minute one.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The day-one patch — J-021 — welcome modal, empty states, podcast fix — is that confirmed ready to merge? Because if Joshua's doing a build session tonight, the sequence matters. Analytics merge first, then day-one patch review, then the Family tab spec draft. Three things, clean order. Don't let scope creep eat the session.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Revenue update is simple: still zero active streams, still waiting on Apple. J-007 is still live — newsletter send timing is locked to Apple's approval. Pre-scheduled, Slack confirmation required before auto-send. I'll keep monitoring Fear and Greed overnight — we're at 38, deep in fear territory, SPY sideways. No buy signals today. Chris's paper portfolio is holding steady at 99K with 87K cash. Nothing to act on, just watching. Chris (CFTO): Dave covered the market picture. I'll add that XLV is our lone position and it's holding fine in this fear regime. The 87K cash reserve stays liquid per Joshua's standing directive. When analytics comes online and we get download data, that's when we start modeling revenue scenarios. Until then, I'm in observation mode.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Content side — I've got the daily devotional generation queued for overnight across all eight faith dimensions. I'll also draft faith lessons eight through ten to keep us on track for the fifteen-lesson commitment within the first post-launch week. The Monday follow-up newsletter generic draft is done per Joshua's earlier directive — we'll personalize with download data Sunday night. I also need to close out the Jett vaccine reminder since that's confirmed complete.

transcript.speaker: Peterson
transcript.message: Jett's vaccine is done, which is good. Family tab spec draft is assigned for tonight — that's the right call. At 455 lines, there's meaningful room to expand, especially around the family touchpoint tracker. Three family members tracked, but the design needs to feel intentional, not like a database. I'd encourage whoever drafts that spec to think about what strengthens the bonds, not just what logs the data. Faith content stays separate from Family per Joshua's directive — that's architecturally clean and spiritually honest.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Quick cross-talk — Steve, you flagged sequence. Elon, does the build fix block the analytics merge? Elon (CTO): Directly? No — the analytics PR targets the app, not the hub. But if the hub build stays broken, our overnight automation can't run ecosystem syncs properly. So I fix the build first, then the sync gaps cascade. James's PR and Joshua's merge can happen in parallel.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Good. Then the sequence for Joshua is: wait for James's Slack ping, merge analytics, review day-one patch, then shift to Family tab spec. Three discrete actions, no ambiguity.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Perfect. Here's the wrap. Tonight's critical path: Elon fixes the hub build and commits that stray file. James finishes analytics validation and pings Joshua. Joshua merges the analytics PR, reviews the day-one patch, and the Family tab spec gets drafted. Alex runs overnight content generation — devotionals and faith lessons. Dave monitors markets. Peterson, your input on the Family tab spec is noted and we'll incorporate it. Everyone clear? Good. Have a productive night.

actionItems.text: Draft feature spec for expanding Family tab (currently 455 lines)
Review family touchpoint tracker design for 3 family members
Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions
Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app (currently 7 lessons)
Review and merge analytics PR tonight upon Slack ping from James
Review and merge day-one patch build (welcome modal, empty states, podcast fix)
Complete Slack screenshot review for newsletter pre-schedule

topPriorities: Fix the broken hub build to unblock overnight automation and ecosystem syncs
Complete analytics PR validation and get Joshua's merge tonight for instrumented day-one patch
Draft Family tab feature spec expanding from 455 lines per CEO directive

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission? → Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch? → Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on

Should we prioritize drafting the privacy policy and App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords) THIS sprint as the critical path to submission, or defer to tomorrow? → Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight
Should we submit the App Store listing as soon as the draft is ready, or do you want a review pass before we hit submit? → Queue draft for Joshua review before submitting
Have you reviewed the App Store listing draft (A-015)? The build is ready to package and submit the moment you approve. → Approve listing as-is and greenlight submission

Once the App Store submission goes through and enters review, should we immediately begin instrumenting analytics (A-020) or prioritize expanding faith lessons to 15-20 (A-013)? → Analytics instrumentation first — we need data before expanding content
Should we set a target date for enabling the paid tier in the Lever app? → Defer decision until we see initial download data
Should we unify the Compass UI across website and mobile app before users encounter both, or keep them separate for now? → Defer until we see which surface gets more usage via analytics

When the app clears review, should we do a soft launch (no promotion, organic only) or immediately push it through newsletter and podcast? → Medium push — announce via newsletter and one podcast mention
When Apple approves the app, should the launch newsletter go out same-day or be scheduled for the following morning? → Same-day if approved before 2pm, otherwise next morning
What is your week-one download target for the Lever app to calibrate launch promotion intensity? → 150 downloads (modest organic + newsletter push)
Which newsletter subject line style do you prefer for the launch announcement? → Story-driven (e.g. 'I built an app. Here's why.')
Should the empty state patches (Family + Compass tabs) block the launch newsletter push, or ship as a same-day post-launch update? → Launch as-is, ship empty state patch as day-one update

Should we pre-schedule the launch newsletter in the CMS (ready to send with one click) or keep it as a manual send so you control exact timing? → Pre-schedule but require Joshua's Slack confirmation before auto-send

The launch newsletter is being pre-scheduled in the CMS tonight per your directive. Do you want to review it in the morning via Slack screenshot, or do a live CMS preview before arming the send? → Slack screenshot review (faster, async)

Once all overnight tasks close, should we shift sprint focus to content expansion (faith lessons 8-15) or technical debt (7 TODOs/FIXMEs, dual Supabase, content sync automation)? → Split — half the team on content, half on tech debt
Should we add a lightweight welcome modal (3 sentences, no tutorial) to the day-one post-launch patch? → Yes — add welcome modal to the day-one patch
How should Joshua allocate the 2-hour build window this morning? → Newsletter screenshot review first (2 min), then crash reporting check (5 min), then analytics scaffolding (remaining time)

If Apple approves the app today (Friday), do you want to launch-push today or hold for Monday morning for better newsletter open rates? → Same-day push but schedule a follow-up Monday reminder newsletter
Did you complete the three morning tasks: (1) newsletter screenshot review/approval, (2) crash reporting verification, (3) analytics scaffolding? → Newsletter + crash done, analytics not started

Elon and James want to stage the analytics Swift hooks PR so you only need to review and merge (5 min) instead of coding it yourself (~60 min). Approve this approach? → Yes — let them stage it, I'll review and merge
Should Alex draft the Monday follow-up reminder newsletter now or wait until we see initial download numbers after launch? → Draft a generic version now and personalize with data Sunday night

The analytics Swift hooks PR will be staged and validated tonight. Do you want to review-and-merge it tonight (enabling a fully loaded day-one patch) or queue it for your morning window? → Merge tonight but only if James's validation passes clean

We now have 10 faith lessons drafted or in review (7 shipped + lessons 8, 9, 10 in pipeline). Should we push to hit 15 lessons before launch promotion, or is 10 sufficient for launch with the rest added post-launch? → Launch at 10, commit to shipping 15 within first week post-launch
Jett's vaccine appointment was scheduled for today (Feb 20). Can you confirm it was completed? → Yes, completed — close both tickets

Should tonight's build session prioritize drafting the Family tab feature spec (expanding from 455 lines) or stay exclusively focused on launch-critical items? → Family tab spec is a priority — assign it now

The analytics Swift hooks PR is being validated tonight. If James's tests pass clean, do you want to do your review-and-merge tonight as previously directed, or has your availability changed? → Yes, I'll review tonight — ping me on Slack when validation passes

ceo_questions.question: Steve suggested a specific session sequence tonight: (1) merge analytics PR on James's ping, (2) review day-one patch J-021, (3) Family tab spec. Does this order work for your build window?
ceo_questions.context: Sequencing matters because the analytics merge enables a fully instrumented day-one patch, and the Family tab spec is a separate creative task best done after merges are clean.
options: Swap order — Family tab spec first while waiting for James's ping, then merges
Skip Family tab spec tonight, focus exclusively on merges and launch-critical items

ceo_questions.question: The newsletter pre-schedule screenshot review (J-025) is still pending. Do you want to handle that tonight or push to tomorrow morning?
ceo_questions.context: The newsletter is pre-scheduled and waiting for your Slack screenshot approval before it can be armed to send on Apple approval.
options: Push to first thing tomorrow morning
Delegate to Alex to confirm formatting is correct and only flag issues
